benchmark_title: Memory OS Swap Recovery Completeness Benchmark

scenario_a_chunk_roundtrip.name: Single Chunk Roundtrip Integrity
scenario_a_chunk_roundtrip.key_insight: swap_out -> swap_in path is fully lossless with zlib compression

scenario_b_compression_retention.name: Compaction Information Retention (Single Round)
scenario_b_compression_retention.key_issue: checkpoint_restore() returns only latest checkpoint, max_hit_ids limits recovery

scenario_c_multi_compaction_loss.name: Multi-Round Compaction Cumulative Loss
scenario_c_multi_compaction_loss.key_issue: consumed=1 flag prevents checkpoint reuse across multiple compaction rounds

root_causes.issue: Checkpoint mechanism designed for CRIU, not suitable for Swap Recovery
root_causes.impact: Multi-round compaction fails after 1st round; max_hit_ids truncates large worksets

root_causes.issue: Content field not stored in checkpoint table
root_causes.impact: Only summary preserved (49%), content preservation rate at 13.9%
2nd+ compaction rounds get zero recovery; need multi-use semantics

short_term: Fix checkpoint consumption to support multi-round recovery
Extend swap_state.json with full_hit_ids and content_summary fields
Improve resume-task-state.py to prioritize swap_state.json over checkpoint_restore
medium_term: Separate CRIU and Swap Recovery mechanisms into distinct tables
Implement Governor-aware adaptive recovery (LOW/NORMAL/HIGH/CRITICAL pressure levels)
long_term: Enable secondary compression (brotli/lz4) for swap data
Implement mmap + eBPF hybrid memory management for near-native swap performance
